an1.258:1.1: "This is the foremost, *bhikkhus*, among my female disciple lay women who first went for refuge — namely, Sujātā, the daughter of Seniya.
an1.259:1.1: … Among donors — namely, Visākhā, Migāra's mother.
an1.260:1.1: … Among those of great learning — namely, Khujjuttarā.
an1.261:1.1: … Among those who dwell in *mettā* — namely, Sāmāvatī.
an1.262:1.1: … Among meditators — namely, Uttarā, Nanda's mother.
an1.263:1.1: … Among those who offer excellent gifts — namely, Suppavāsā, the daughter of the Koliyas.
an1.264:1.1: … Among those who attend on the sick — namely, the lay woman Suppiyā.
an1.265:1.1: … Among those with confirmed confidence — namely, Kātiyānī.
an1.266:1.1: … Among those who are intimate — namely, Nakulamātā, the householder's wife.
an1.267:1.1: … Among those with confidence through oral tradition — namely, the lay woman Kāḷī of Kuraraghara."
